themes.label: 2Y Mispricing Imminent Disinflation
themes.stance: 2Y at 4.13% — ~50bp above funds — prices a hawkish-hold-to-hikes path that an oil collapse (Iran deal) directly undercuts; core CPI benign at +0.21% MoM May; tactical long duration into July 14 CPI.
themes.tell: WTI sustained sub-90 + July 14 headline CPI soft print confirms; 2Y falling back toward/below 3.63% funds signals the market is pricing cuts. A core CPI re-acceleration above +0.3% MoM or WTI snap back above 100 kills the trade.

themes.label: Iran Deal → Oil Collapse → Disinflation
themes.stance: Iran–US peace framework unconfirmed but already collapsing WTI to multi-month lows; Hormuz reopening + sanctions lift + OPEC+ Jun 22 creates double-bearish crude setup; energy reversal is the mechanism that flips headline CPI dovish into July.
themes.tell: Iran deal formally confirmed + WTI holds below 90 through OPEC+ Jun 22 meeting; deal collapse or OPEC+ production cut would negate.

themes.label: Asset Euphoria vs. Collapsing Consumer Confidence
themes.stance: SpaceX IPO (largest debut ever) and Barron's-flagged 'hopes and dreams' tape coexist with Michigan sentiment at 49.8 (from 56.6 in Feb); nominal spend holds but the divergence between consumer action and consumer feeling is widening — froth signal not yet a timing tool.
themes.tell: Michigan sentiment further deterioration below 47 or retail sales MoM turning negative would confirm the crack; SpaceX/IPO halo names holding gains sustains the froth narrative.

themes.label: Secular Defense Rearmament Bid
themes.stance: Senate backs 'Department of War' rename + $750M Ukraine aid; UK defense secretary resigned over insufficient spending; structural rearmament demand is independent of Gulf de-escalation — Iran calm does not dent the secular bid.
themes.tell: NATO/EU defense budget commitments rising above 2.5% GDP targets or additional US supplemental defense appropriations confirm; a comprehensive peace settlement in Ukraine would be the key refutation signal.

themes.label: El Niño Food & Cat-Insurance Risk
themes.stance: 'Very strong' El Niño officially declared; China warns of extreme floods threatening rice crop; soft-commodity and P&C catastrophe risk appearing underpriced by equity tape — small long DBA, trim P&C cat exposure.
themes.tell: USDA or FAO crop damage estimates for Asian rice/wheat; P&C reinsurer guidance cuts or cat-loss warnings in Q2 earnings would confirm the underpricing thesis.

themes.label: AI Capex Durability After Oracle Stumble
themes.stance: Oracle slid post-earnings — a crack in the AI-capex-beneficiary trade; Apple concedes AI lead to Google via Siri/Gemini rebuild (WWDC); the full read awaits late-July mega-cap/semi prints (MSFT/GOOGL/META, NVDA/AMD).
themes.tell: NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL late-July earnings capex guidance — sustained or raised AI spend confirms durability; a second miss or guidance cut after Oracle confirms the crack is spreading.

themes.label: Divided Government → Fiscal Gridlock & Supply Risk
themes.stance: 2026 midterms prediction markets point to Dem House (82.5%) / R Senate (55.5%) — structural fiscal gridlock ahead, no fresh stimulus; late-July Treasury quarterly refunding is the near-term long-end supply risk that keeps the duration add tactical not structural.
themes.tell: Late-July Treasury refunding announcement size and 10Y/30Y auction tail; 10Y yield breaking above 4.75% on supply indigestion would confirm the long-end risk.

themes.label: Core Inflation Trajectory — Benign or Re-Accelerating
themes.stance: Core CPI +0.21% MoM May (vs +0.38% April) is benign but one soft month is not a trend; core PCE +0.24% in April; the entire disinflation thesis hinges on Jun 26 core PCE and Jul 14 core CPI not re-accelerating.
themes.tell: Jun 26 core PCE print: another +0.3%+ MoM rebuts 'benign, not accelerating' and undercuts the disinflation lede; a second consecutive sub-0.25% read confirms the downtrend.
